reasoning: I attempted to fetch the FRED macro series but there was no data available. I therefore relied on web searches to gauge the current rate environment. Searching for 2025 Treasury and corporate yield trends revealed that as of April/May 2025, yields have been facing upward pressure due to sticky inflation concerns and higher-than-expected tariffs announced recently, which caused 'significant movements' upwards in asset yields. Consequently, experts have revised their end-of-2025 fed funds and Treasury yield expectations higher, indicating a current upward momentum. Because of this recent upward trend, I assign a slightly >0.5 probability (54%) of the yield increasing from its May 11 level in the short term (May 18). Following the instruction to increase uncertainty (bringing probabilities closer to 50%) for more distant horizons, I taper the probabilities down toward 0.51 for the November 7 outcome, reflecting the unpredictable nature of longer-term rate trajectory amid expected, but delayed, Fed rate cuts.
